name: ask-slack-vscode
description: Ask user questions with local UI and Slack DM fallback for Copilot Chat agents

languageModelTools.name: askSlack_askUser
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Ask the user a question. Shows a local UI prompt immediately; if the user does not respond within a configurable timeout (default 60 s), the question is forwarded to their Slack DM. Supports multiple-choice options (single or multi-select) just like vscode_askQuestions. Use this tool when you need human input, clarification, or approval.

question.description: The question to ask the user

context.description: Optional context to provide with the question

options.description: Optional list of selectable answers to show as buttons

recommended.description: Mark this option as the recommended default

multiSelect.description: When true, the user can select multiple options. Defaults to false (single-select).

allowFreeformInput.description: When true, the user can type a custom answer in addition to selecting options. Defaults to true when no options are provided.

askSlack.awayMode.markdownDescription:
When enabled, questions are sent directly to Slack without waiting for a local answer.

💡 **API key and full settings:** [Open Ask Slack Settings Panel](command:askSlack.openSettings)

askSlack.timeoutSeconds.markdownDescription:
Seconds to wait for a local answer before sending the question to Slack.

💡 **API key and full settings:** [Open Ask Slack Settings Panel](command:askSlack.openSettings)

askSlack.apiUrl.markdownDescription:
Base URL of the ask-slack-mcp HTTP server (e.g. `http://localhost:3000`).

💡 **API key cannot be set here** — use the [Ask Slack Settings Panel](command:askSlack.openSettings) (stored securely in OS keychain).

askSlack.slackUserId.markdownDescription:
Slack user ID to send DMs to.

💡 **API key and full settings:** [Open Ask Slack Settings Panel](command:askSlack.openSettings)
